perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Prospective Vendor specif ied in Section 3 above shall not apply, and the Prospectiv e Vendor shall have no further obligations, with respect to any Proprietary Inform ation to the extent Prospective Vendor can demonstrate, by clear and convincing evidence, that such Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Prospective Vendor specif ied in Section 3 above shall not apply, and the Prospectiv e Vendor shall have no further obligations, with respect to any Proprietary Inform ation to the extent Prospective Vendor can demonstrate, by a preponderance of the evidence, that such Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: Lowering the burden of proof needed for an exception to the non-disclosure obligations from 'clear and convincing evidence' to 'preponderance of the evidence' in Section 4 creates a contradiction with Section 3. Section 3 stipulates strict responsibility for disclosure, whereas Section 4 allows for easier exceptions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure of Proprietary Information . The Prospective Vendor shall hold in confidence, and shall not disclose to any person or entity, any Proprietary Information. The Prospective Vendor shall use such Proprietary Infor mation only for the purpose for which it was disclosed and shall not use or exploit such Pro prietary Information for his own benefit or the benefit of another without the prior written consent of the Company. Without limitation of the foregoing, the Prospective Vendor shall not cause or permit reverse engineering of any Proprietary Information or decom pilation or disassembly of any software programs which are part of the Proprietary Information. The Prospective Vendor shall be responsible to the Company for any disclos ure or misuse of Proprietary Information which results from a failure to comply with this provision. The Prospective Vendor will promptly report to the Company any actu al or suspected violation of the terms of this Agreement and will take all reasonable furt her steps requested by the Company to prevent, control or remedy any such violation.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'clear and convincing evidence' for an exception to non-disclosure, setting a high bar. The changed text lowers this to 'preponderance of the evidence', making it easier to claim an exception. This directly contradicts the strict confidentiality obligations in the 'Contradicted Text' which mandates responsibility for any disclosure and requires preventative measures. The lower burden of proof now allows for easier circumvention of these strict obligations, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Prospective Vendor agrees that the Company is and shall remain the exclusive owner of the Proprietary Information and all patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and othe r intellectual property rights therein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Prospective Vendor agrees that the Company is and shall remain the exclusive owner of the Proprietary Information, except for methodologies independently developed by the Prospective Vendor during the evaluation period, and all patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and othe r intellectual property rights therein.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except for methodologies independently developed by the Prospective Vendor during the evaluation period' introduces a contradiction. Section 5 initially states that the Company is the 'exclusive owner' of all Proprietary Information, but this change allows the vendor to potentially own some related intellectual property. This contradicts the definition of 'exclusive owner'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Proprietary Information . The Prospective Vendor agrees that the Company is and shall remain the exclusive owner of the Proprietary Information, except for methodologies independently developed by the Prospective Vendor during the evaluation period, and all patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and othe r intellectual property rights therein. No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Pro spective Vendor is granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the 'exclusive ownership' clause, stating the Company owns the Proprietary Information 'except for methodologies independently developed by the Prospective Vendor during the evaluation period'. This directly contradicts the initial statement of exclusive ownership, making the ownership ambiguous and creating a clear in-text contradiction. The contradicted text which is identical to the changed text reinforces the contradiction, as it includes the contradictory exception within the ownership clause itself.

perturbation.original_text: The Prospective Vendor shall, upon the terminati on of this Agreement or at the request of the Company, promptl y destroy or return to the Company all drawings, documents and other tangible manifest ations of Proprietary Information received by the Prospective vendor pursuant to this Agreement (and all copies and reproductions thereof).
perturbation.changed_text: The Prospective Vendor shall, upon the terminati on of this Agreement or at the request of the Company, make a reasonable effort to destroy or return to the Company all drawings, documents and other tangible manifest ations of Proprietary Information received by the Prospective vendor pursuant to this Agreement (and all copies and reproductions thereof).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'promptly destroy or return' to 'make a reasonable effort to destroy or return' weakens the obligation and introduces ambiguity. The original text is clear in mandating destruction/return, while the modified text introduces a subjective standard ('reasonable effort'). This contradicts the strict confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 3, by making the return or destruction of proprietary information no longer a mandatory step.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure of Proprietary Information . The Prospective Vendor shall hold in confidence, and shall not disclose to any person or entity, any Proprietary Information. The Prospective Vendor shall use such Proprietary Infor mation only for the purpose for which it was disclosed and shall not use or exploit such Pro prietary Information for his own benefit or the benefit of another without the prior written consent of the Company. Without limitation of the foregoing, the Prospective Vendor shall not cause or permit reverse engineering of any Proprietary Information or decom pilation or disassembly of any software programs which are part of the Proprietary Information. The Prospective Vendor shall be responsible to the Company for any disclos ure or misuse of Proprietary Information which results from a failure to comply with this provision. The Prospective Vendor will promptly report to the Company any actu al or suspected violation of the terms of this Agreement and will take all reasonable furt her steps requested by the Company to prevent, control or remedy any such violation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the obligation to destroy or return proprietary information from a mandatory 'promptly destroy or return' to a subjective 'make a reasonable effort to destroy or return'. This introduces ambiguity and contradicts the strict confidentiality obligations in the contradicted text, which requires the Prospective Vendor to 'hold in confidence, and shall not disclose to any person or entity, any Proprietary Information' and makes them responsible for any 'disclosure or misuse of Proprietary Information which results from a failure to comply with this provision'. The 'reasonable effort' clause now permits a possible failure to comply, creating a direct contradiction with the requirement to hold information in confidence and be responsible for its disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain effective with respect to any Proprietary Information which is disclosed herein at any time with three (3) years f rom the date first dated above, except for the obligations of the parties hereto with respect to Proprietary Information received prior to such termination which shall survive such termin ation.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain effective with respect to any Proprietary Information which is disclosed herein at any time with three (3) years f rom the date first dated above, and the obligations of the parties hereto with respect to Proprietary Information received prior to such termination shall also terminate after three years.
perturbation.explanation: The edited text removes the survival clause for obligations related to Proprietary Information post-termination, establishing the obligations of the parties with respect to Proprietary Information terminates after the 3 years. In effect, the initial text states that the agreement's confidentiality will remain effective even after the agreement terminates, and the modified one says the agreement's confidentiality will terminate three years after the agreement date, creating an **in-text contradiction**.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous (d) The provisions of this Agreement are necessary for the protection of the business and goodwill of the Company and are consid ered by the Prospective Vendor to be reasonable for such purpos e. The Prospective Vendor agrees that any breach of this Agreement wil l cause the Company substantial and irreparable damages and, therefore, in the event of any such breach, in addition to other remedies which may be available, the Company shall have the right to seek specific performance a nd other injunctive and equitable relief. This Agreement shall remain effective with respect to any Proprietary Information which is disclosed herein at any time with three (3) years f rom the date first dated above, and the obligations of the parties hereto with respect to Proprietary Information received prior to such termination shall also terminate after three years. PCA Technology Limited _________________________________ Signed By: _____________________________ Title:_____________________________ Prospective Vendor (please print name) _________________________________ Signed By: Title: _________________________________ Company Stamp
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement's confidentiality obligations for Proprietary Information survive termination. The changed text specifies that these obligations terminate after three years, contradicting the original provision. The changed text is also included within the 'Contradicted Text' section, creating a direct, strong in-text contradiction within the document. This follows the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction', as the inconsistent obligations create an in-text contradiction.
